user_query:
I want to plan next week’s community shares for my 3D printed figurines tips and recommendations. Please use my local inputs and a bit of web research to produce a concrete weekly plan and a brief status update I can post to my group.

Inputs to use from the workspace:
- input/availability.yaml (my timezone, week start, and time windows)
- input/topics.jsonl (candidate post topics with tags, estimated durations, and whether they need research)
- input/priorities.json (tags to emphasize or de-prioritize this week)
- input/research_targets.csv (creators/lines I want to check for upcoming or notable releases)

Required deliverables and paths:
1) outputs/schedule/calendar.csv
   - One row per scheduled slot for the week defined in input/availability.yaml.
   - Columns: date, start_time_local, end_time_local, slot_type (post|research|maintenance), ref_id (topic_id or target_id or "-"), title, tags (semicolon-separated if post), eta_minutes (if post else "-"), notes.
   - Use the availability windows exactly (localize to the timezone in availability.yaml). Avoid overlaps. Fill earliest windows first.
   - Select up to rules.max_posts posts for the week. Prefer topics that include any tag in priorities.json:emphasize_tags; break ties by smaller eta_minutes. Do not schedule topics whose eta_minutes exceed the window length. If no post fits a given window, assign the slot to research (see #2) or a maintenance topic (a topic with a "maintenance" tag) if available.
   - Ensure at least rules.min_research_sessions total research slots across the week. Assign each research slot to a specific target_id from input/research_targets.csv (round-robin is fine) and set slot_type to "research" with ref_id=target_id.

2) outputs/research/research_log.jsonl
   - Perform live search engine queries (no direct URLs provided here) for each target in input/research_targets.csv using the target name and keywords from the file. For each target_id, run at least two distinct queries and visit at least two distinct domains that appear to be official or creator-run sources or widely recognized storefront/community platforms (e.g., patreon.com, myminifactory.com, gumroad.com, manufacturer/creator domains). Do not depend on any single result.
   - Write one JSON object per visited relevant page with fields: {"target_id", "query", "domain", "page_title", "retrieval_timestamp_iso", "note"}. The note should be a one-sentence relevance summary (e.g., "catalog page listing dragon busts" or "monthly release post"). Do not include full page text.
   - Ensure each target_id has entries covering at least two distinct domains.

3) outputs/status_update.md
   - A concise, readable summary I can share internally that includes:
     • Inputs detected: list the files you found under input/ (filenames) so I can double-check we’re aligned.
     • Plan overview: the reasoning behind topic choices (how emphasize_tags influenced selection) and how many posts vs research sessions were scheduled.
     • Scheduled posts: bullet list with date/time (local), topic title, tags, and eta.
     • Research focus: for each target_id, a bullet list of source domains and page titles you recorded (domain + title only, no URLs).
     • Next steps/deferrals: note any topics that didn’t fit due to time constraints.

Scheduling and verification constraints:
- Interpret week_start_date and timezone from input/availability.yaml. Schedule only within the provided windows for that specific week. Times in outputs must be local to that timezone.
- Respect rules.max_posts and rules.min_research_sessions from availability.yaml.
- A topic can be scheduled at most once. Only schedule topics that fit within a window’s duration (eta_minutes <= window length in minutes). If a topic has needs_research=true, include that as a note; research will be handled by the dedicated research slots, not tied to the post slot timing.
- The research_log.jsonl must include at least two distinct domains per target_id. Do not paste URLs in status_update.md; cite each source by domain and page title.
- All three outputs must be created at the exact paths specified.

Please inspect the input directory to confirm what files and contents are present before planning. Use a search engine for the research targets; avoid hardcoding assumptions. Thanks! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
